$schema_note: Canonical sprint brief shape for Sanders Intelligence micro-sprints. Replace every <PLACEHOLDER> and keep every key. Do not add or remove top-level keys without updating SKILL.md.

project.name: Sanders intelligence app
project.stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, Tailwind, React Router v6, TanStack Query v5, Supabase, Recharts

selection_rationale: <2-3 sentences: why these picks, why these dates, what got deprioritized>
instructions_for_implementer: Work tasks in the 'order' field. For EACH task: read 'clarifying_questions' first and ask the user before writing code. Treat 'likely_files' as starting points for investigation, not the full surface. Implement against 'requirements', validate against 'completion_criteria', and write/extend tests per 'test_coverage'. When done, update the matching Asana task's completion status and leave a comment citing the file/line evidence.

tasks.title: <task title>
tasks.outline: <one paragraph: what the user-visible change is and why it matters now>
tasks.technical_description: <where it lives in code, what already exists, what existing patterns to reuse>
likely_files: <path/to/file.tsx> - <what to look at, approx line range>
expected_outcomes: <the user-facing payoff in one or two sentences>
clarifying_questions: <question the implementer should ask the user before any code is written>
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

sprint_close_checklist: All sprint tasks marked completed in Asana with a comment citing file paths and line numbers.
All new tests pass: cd app && npm test.
Run cd app && npm run dev and smoke-test the affected pages end-to-end.
Update CLAUDE.md if any new module or convention was added.
Update memory (project_sanders_intelligence.md) if the architecture changed.
